new_classes.definition_en: Sparkling wine of fresh grapes falling under CN subheading 2204 10 96, being a sparkling wine other than Champagne (2204 10 11), Cava (2204 10 91), Prosecco (2204 10 93), other PDO sparkling wine (2204 10 94), and Asti Spumante/Moscato d'Asti (2204 10 95), classified under heading 2204 10 as defined by subposition note 1 to Chapter 22.

coverage_explanation: The legal text for 22041096 is very brief, referring only to "Schaumwein" (sparkling wine) within the range 22041011–22041098 and pointing to subposition note 1 of Chapter 22. The note itself does not appear in full here, but based on the CN hierarchy context, 22041096 is a residual "other sparkling wine" subheading that captures sparkling wines not classified under sibling codes (Champagne 22041011, Cava 22041091, Prosecco 22041093, other PDO sparkling wine 22041094, Asti Spumante/Moscato d'Asti 22041095). The proposed axioms capture: (1) subclass of SparklingWine; (2) complement restrictions excluding ChampagneRegion and ProseccoDesignationRegion production regions; (3) complement restriction excluding PDO quality. However, the full text of subposition note 1 (defining the pressure thresholds and other criteria for sparkling wine) is not reproduced in the provided legal notes, so the pressure criterion (excess pressure ≥ 3 bar at 20 °C) that defines sparkling wine as a category is inherited from the parent class SparklingWine but not explicitly re-stated here. Additionally, fine-grained sibling exclusions (Cava, Asti Spumante/Moscato d'Asti) cannot be fully modelled without their region/quality classes being defined in the TBox. Coverage is therefore partial.
